Notes: 1st gen causes sedation because they are able to penetrate CNS. Promethazine has similar structure to phenothiazine antipsychotics therefore may cause corneal keratopathy, lenticular changes and pigmentary retinopathy.

Blocks nerve conduction and changes membrane permeability by inhibiting the influx of Na. Prevents nerve from depolarizing.

Topical anesthetics are esters because these have shorter duration of action!!

S/E: corneal melt with excessive use

Trade Name: Inderal

Drug Class: Beta Blocker

MOA: Non-selective B1 and B2 antagonists. Also block renin from kidney.

Use: Hypertension.

S/E: lower IOP, depression, bradycardia, arrhythmia, wheezing, bronchospasm, and erectile dysfunction.

Trade Name: Mestinon

Drug Class: Acetylcholinesterase Inhibitors (indirect cholinergic agonists)

MOA: Inhibits acetylcholinesterase, thereby increasing Ach in synaptic cleft.

Use: Tx of myasthenia gravis.

Dosage: 60mg tablets QID

Trade Name: Duraprim

MOA: Inhibit Dihydrofolate reductase, used in second step of folic acid synthesis.

Use:Bacteriostatic. G(+) and G(-). Used for Toxo.

Side Effects: Orals --> bone marrow suppression --> aplastic anemia.

Trade Name: Lucentis

Drug Class: Agents for Exudative ARMD

MOA: Monoclonal antibody (Fab portion) that binds to and inhibits VEGF.

Use: AMD. Anti-VEGF.

Notes: Intravitreal injection.

Trade Name: Zantac

Drug Class: H2 Antagonist

MOA: Binds to H2 receptor of gastric parietal cells, preventing histamine induced HCl secretion.

Use: PUD. Healing and preventing stomach ulcers and acid reflux.

S/E: Diarrhea.

Trade Name: Rifadin

Drug Class: TB Meds

MOA: Binds to beta subunit of RNA polymerase and inhibits transcription.

Use: Can be used as mono therapy for latent TB.

S/E: hepatotoxicity, pink coloration of tears and urine.

Trade Name: Vexol

Drug Class: Corticosteroid

MOA: Inhibits phospholipase A2.

Use: Anti-inflammatory and immunosuppressive.

S/E: Delayed healing, PSC, increased risk of secondary infections, potential to cause diabetes, and glaucoma.

Note: Strong steroid!

Drug Class: Cholinergic Antagonists

MOA: Block Ach at muscarinic sites in ciliary body and iris.

Use: Cycloplegic refractions, pupillary dilation, and management of uveitis.

S/E: Penetrates BBB therefore CNS effects more likely. Hallucinations, amnesia, confusion, etc. CI in Down's Syndrome!

Note: Not used in glaucoma tx!

Dosage: BID-TID for uveitis

Trade Name: Viagra

Drug Class: Phosphodiesterase (PDE) inhibitors.

MOA: Inhibit PDE-5 thereby increasing blood flow to penis via relaxation of blood vessel smooth muscle.

Use: Erectile dysfunction.

S/E: flushing, HA's, sustained erection, NAION, color defects (cyanopsia), and photosensitivity.

Trade Name: Zocor

Drug Class: HMG CoA Reductase Inhibitor

MOA: Inhibits HMG CoA Reductase, an enzyme used for synthesis of cholesterol. Lowers LDL and TG's, and raises HDL.

Use: First line therapy for hyperlipidemia. Hypercholesterolemia too.

S/E: Hepatotoxicity, myopathy. CI in pregnancy and lactation.

Trade Name: Muro-128

Drug Class: Hyperosmotic Agent

MOA: Hypertonic solution.

Use: Reduces corneal edema.

Notes: Drops or ointment.

Dosage: drops QID and ung qhs for Fuch's.

Trade Name: Aldactone

Drug Class: Diuretic (Potassium Sparing)

MOA: Blocks aldosterones action at the late DCT and collecting duct thereby increasing excretion of sodium and water and decreases secretion of potassium.

Use: Hypertension

S/E: hyperkalemia, gynecomastia, ANTIANDROGEN EFFECTS.

Trade Name: Carafate

Drug Class: Gastric Lining Protector

MOA: Forms a "paste" and coats damaged ulcer tissue.

Use: Protect stomach lining from acid. Acute management of PUD.

S/E: May interfere with absorption of oral meds. Take 2 hours after other meds.

Trade Name: Sulfamyd

Drug Class: Folic Acid Inhibitors

MOA: Inhibit Dyhydropteroate synthase, first step of folic acid synthesis.

Use:Bacteriostatic. G(+) and G(-).

Side Effects: Kernicterus in infants, myopic shift, SJS.

Trade Name: Macrosulfan

Drug Class: Folic Acid Inhibitors

MOA: Inhibit Dyhydropteroate synthase, first step of folic acid synthesis.

Use:Bacteriostatic. G(+) and G(-).

Side Effects: Kernicterus in infants, myopic shift, SJS.

Trade Name: Gantanol

Drug Class: Folic Acid Inhibitors

MOA: Inhibit Dyhydropteroate synthase, first step of folic acid synthesis.

Use:Bacteriostatic. G(+) and G(-). Used with Trimethoprim in Toxo (Bactrim).

Side Effects: Kernicterus in infants, myopic shift, SJS.

Trade Name: Gantrisin

Drug Class: Folic Acid Inhibitors

MOA: Inhibit Dyhydropteroate synthase, first step of folic acid synthesis.

Use:Bacteriostatic. G(+) and G(-).

Side Effects: Kernicterus in infants, myopic shift, SJS.

Trade Name: Imitrex

Drug Class: Migraine therapy

MOA: Serotonin receptor 1B and 1 D agonists. Causes vasoconstriction of cranial blood vessels and reduces inflammation in CNS.

Use: Migraines.

S/E: Systemic vasoconstriction causing NAION, MI, strokes. CI in patients with hx of strokes.

Trade Name: Sumycin

Drug Class: Tetracylcines

MOA: Protein synthesis inhibitor at 30s ribosomal subunit.

Use: Bacteriostatic. G(+) and G(-).

S/E: Pseudotumor, bone growth retardation, discolouring of teeth.

Notes: Contraindicated in renal failure, pregnancy and children. Dairy in GI tract may inhibit absorption.

Trade Name: Visine

Drug Class: Adrenergic Agonists

MOA: Alpha agonists (some beta too)

Use: Constrict conjunctival blood vessels.

S/E: Long term use may result in fixed dilated pupil due to overstimulation of Alpha1 on dilator.

Trade Name: Mellaril

Drug Class: Phenothiazine (antipsychotic)

MOA: Dopamine recepter antagonist.

Use: Schizophrenia

S/E: Pigment on corneal endo, ASC, hyperpigmentation of retina, anticholinergic effects, oculogyric crisis, and potential Parkinson-like effects.

Trade Name: Timoptic, Betimol

Drug Class: Adrenergic Antagonists (B blocker)

MOA: Non-selective B receptor blocker.

Use: Lowering IOP in glaucoma.

S/E: Caution in diabetics as it may mask symptoms of hypoglycemia. may also mask hyperthyroid symptoms. Weakness can be exacerbated in MG.

Notes: Most effective at lowering IOP (25%). Morning single dose is recommended. Has crossover effect (untreated eye benefits too).

Dosage: BID

Trade Name: Tobrex

Drug Class: Aminoglycoside

MOA: Protein synthesis inhibitor at 30S unit of bacterial ribosome.

Use: Good G(+), but better G(-).

S/E: Topicals cause SPK. Orals can cause ototoxicity and nephrotoxicity.

Notes: Gentamicin too (not on list). TobraGent30!

Dosage: q4h

Trade Name: Tobradex

Combination drug.

Dosage: QID

Trade Name: Topamax

Drug Class: Anticonvulsant

MOA: Multiple CNS effects to prevent seizures

Use: Seizures and migraines.

S/E: Choroidal effusion leading to secondary angle closure glaucoma.

Trade Name: Travatan Z

Drug Class: Prostaglandin Analog

MOA: PGA's act on FP receptors on ciliary muscle, causing reduction of neighbouring collagen via MMP's, which decreases the resistance in the uveoscleral meshwork. Increase uveoscleral outflow. Eyelash growth too.

Use: First line of tx for POAG. 27-35% IOP lowering, the highest of all glaucoma drugs.

S/E: Heterochromia, skin darkening around eyes, and growth of lashes. CI in CME, and active uveitis/inflammation.

Notes: Bedtime dosing recommended.

Dosage: QHS

Trade Name: Kenalog, Aristocort

Drug Class: Anti-inflammatory (Steroids)

MOA: Inhibits phospholipase A2.

Use: Diabetic edema, Grave's disease posterior uveitis, chalazion, and CME.

S/E: Endophthalmitis with injection. Can also cause depigmentation of the eyelid in dark skinned patients. Also PSC, increased IOP, possible diabetes. and immune suppression.

Trade Name: Dyrenium

Drug Class: Diuretics

MOA: Directly blocks ENaCs in late DCT and late collecting ducts.

Notes: Similar to Spironolactone but no anti-androgen effects here.

Drug Class: Folic Acid Inhibitors

MOA: Inhibit Dihydrofolate reductase, used in second step of folic acid synthesis.

Use:Bacteriostatic. G(+) and G(-). Not effective against pseudomonas. Used with Sulfamethoxazole in Toxo (Bactrim).

Side Effects: Orals --> bone marrow suppression --> aplastic anemia.

Trade Name: Polytrim

Drug Class: Antibiotic

MOA: Combo antibiotic. Cell wall synthesis inhibition and folic acid synthesis inhibition.

Use: Bacterial infections.

Dosage: q3h for 7 days

Trade Name: Bactrim

Drug Class: Folic Acid synthesis inhibiors. First and second step.

Use: Toxo and MRSA

Dosage: BID

Drug Class: Cholinergic Antagonists

MOA: Block Ach at muscarinic sites in ciliary body and iris.

Use: Cycloplegic refractions, pupillary dilation, and management of uveitis. Quickest onset and quickest duration of mydriasis.

S/E: Rare, very safe drug.

Note: Not used in glaucoma tx!

Trade Name: Rescula

Drug Class: Prostglandin Analog

MOA: Increase Aq outflow via trabecular meshwork.

Use: POAG and ocular HTN.

Notes: MOA different than other PGA's.

Dosage: QHS

Trade Name: Valtrex

Drug Class: Antiherpes agents

MOA: Inhibits DNA polymerase

Use: All herpes viruses (HSV1-2, VZV, CMV)

S/E: HA's and GI effects are most common.

Dosage: 1000mg TID for 7 days for HZV and 500mg TID for 7 days for HSV. 250mg BID for prophylaxis for HSV keratitis.

Trade Name: Vancocin Pulvules

Drug Class: Antibiotic

Use: Antibiotic of last resort. Can be used in MRSA.

S/E: Ototoxicity, nephrotoxicity.

Trade Name: Levitra

Drug Class: Phosphodiesterase (PDE) inhibitors.

MOA: Inhibit PDE-5 thereby increasing blood flow to penis via relaxation of blood vessel smooth muscle.

Use: Erectile dysfunction.

S/E: flushing, HA's, sustained erection, NAION, color defects (cyanopsia), and photosensitivity.

Trade Name: Calan, Isoptin

Drug Class: Calcium Channel Blockers

MOA: Blocks L-type calcium channels causing a decrease in intracellular calcium, thus decreasing peripheral resistance and blood pressure. Also cause decreased heart rate (chonotropism), and decreased contractility (ionotropism).

Use: Hypertension.

Notes: May worsen or cause heart failure due to direct cardiac effects. Not seen with Nifedipine.

Trade Name: Coumadin

Drug Class: Anticoagulant

MOA: Vitamin K antagonist, thus interfering with clotting factors 2, 7, 9 and 10.

Use: Chronic anticoagulation for patient with previous blood clots, A-Fib, or mechanical heart valves.

Notes: Discontinue for 115 hours prior to cataract surgery. Caution in use with cephalosporins as they block Vit K uptake in GI.

Trade Name: Accolate

Drug Class: Respiratory Agents

MOA: Leukotriene receptor antagonist.

Use: Asthma and bronchconstriction.

Trade Name: Retrovir (AZT)

Drug Class: Antiviral.

MOA: Potent inhibitor of viral RNA-dependent DNA polymerase (aka reverse transcriptase).

Use: Part of 3 drug HIV therapy.

S/E: Bone marrow suppression, lactic acidosis, muscle breakdown, and reported amblyopia and macular edema.

Cholinergic Agonists: increase corneoscleral outflow by pulling on longitudinal muscle of CB which pulls scleral spur and opens TM.

Alpha Agonists: Decrease Aq production via constriction of MACI and increase uveoscleral outflow.

Beta Blockers: Decrease Aq production by inhibiting NPCE.

CAI: Decrease Aq production by inhibiting bicarb formation which impedes Na/Cl transport.

PG's: Increase outflow via uveoscleral pathway via MMP's degrading nearby collagen and reducing resistance in uveosceral route.
